\subheader{SUMMARY}{Customer-focused Senior Data Analyst with 10 years of experience turning complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive product, growth, and monetization decisions in digital products. Proven track record of partnering with product, design, engineering, and executive stakeholders to define KPIs, build reliable reporting, and deliver analysis that changes how teams work. Hands-on with Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Looker, Amplitude, and the full modern analytics stack.

\section{Kano Apps - Casual Games \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/kano_logo.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2025-05 -- \\ 2026-02}
  {Senior Game Data Engineer \& BI Analyst}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Hired to rebuild Kano's reporting platform, migrating RShiny dashboards to AWS Quicksight and enabling analyst self-service on a modern stack. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Reworked, enhanced, and migrated numerous RShiny reports to AWS Quicksight, improving reliability, accessibility, and maintainability.


    \item Improved analyst adoption of the new stack by showcasing workflows, onboarding teammates, and standardizing how reporting was produced and maintained.


    \item Replaced fragile cron-scheduled shell scripts with Prefect workflows, improving orchestration, observability, and analytics stack maintainability.


    \item Selectively applied LLM-assisted code translation to speed up migration.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

\section{\LARGE IC Scope \& Judgment}
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}

~

\textbf{\large Self-Directed Analysis}
\faGem Companion app propensity study was self-proposed — identified the business question, chose the identification strategy, and owned delivery end-to-end without being assigned the work.

~


\textbf{\large Decisions Changed}
\faGem Analysis saved the Web App, drove millions in incremental revenue, and influenced a separate franchise's product roadmap — outcomes, not deliverables.

~


\textbf{\large Scope Beyond Title}
\faGem Trusted with executive board reporting for EA Sports and insight delivery for Motive, BioWare, and DICE studios while a Business Analyst.

~


\textbf{\large Automation at Scale}
\faGem Reduced weeks of manual reporting work to seconds; owned the full pipeline from data to executive consumption.

~


\textbf{\large Methods}
\faGem Propensity score matching, MAB recommendation, churn and spend modelling, A/B test design and readout, LTV forecasting, survival analysis.

\section{Kixeye Inc - Mobile Games \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/kixeye_horse_logo.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2022-02 -- \\ 2025-01}
  {Lead Data Analyst, Core Analytics Team Lead}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Led Kixeye's Core Analytics Team — setting the analytics roadmap, managing analysts, and owning KPI reporting and insight delivery for live game product and leadership teams. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Independently migrated both Kixeye and Babil Games studios' Looker dashboards onto Stillfront's shared instance, reducing studio costs by \$90K annually while improving reporting continuity.


    \item Developed a long-term revenue forecasting model in R and Python used in acquisition assessment, helping identify and correct a \$14M overestimation in a game acquisition deal.


    \item Identified a structural flaw in the matchmaking system driving elevated churn; quantified the affected player population through behavioral segmentation and worked with product and engineering on a redesign that improved retention in impacted cohorts.


    \item Partnered with other Stillfront studios to define KPI frameworks, build reporting, and deliver deeper analysis for game cross-promotion initiatives.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

{\vspace{-0.5cm}}

\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2021-01 -- \\ 2022-02}
  {Senior Product Analyst, Acting Core Analytics Team Lead}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Acting Core Analytics Team Lead, collaborating with product and design teams across multiple live games on KPI definition, A/B reporting, content strategy analysis, and analytics workflows. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Collaborated with the Design team to build a new content cadence strategy backed by funnel analysis and spend trend modeling, resulting in more consistent monetization and reduced revenue volatility across content releases.


    \item Automated KPI forecasting and A/B test reporting ETLs for Product and Design teams, improving experimentation support and freeing substantial analyst time.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

{\vspace{-0.5cm}}

\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2019-08 -- \\ 2021-01}
  {Product Analyst, War Commander: Rogue Assault Team}
  {Vancouver, BC Canada}
  {Product analyst embedded on the War Commander live game team, responsible for player reporting, UA analytics, LTV forecasting, and decision support for product and live ops stakeholders. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Repaired and optimized numerous legacy ETL pipelines and dashboards, producing meaningful warehousing savings and improving trust in core reporting during first three months.


    \item Significantly improved revenue and LTV forecast accuracy used by stakeholders to plan and evaluate product performance.


    \item Proposed a revised user acquisition strategy that helped Facebook campaigns become profitable in 90 days instead of 200.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

\section{Electronic Arts - AAA Games \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/ea_sports.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2018-11 -- \\ 2019-07}
  {Data Scientist, Dynamic Experiences Team}
  {Burnaby, BC Canada}
  {Embedded data scientist on EA Sports' Dynamic Experiences team — designing and evaluating in-game messaging experiments, building player segmentation models, and delivering analysis that improved monetization for FIFA 19 and 20. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Proposed, designed, and evaluated A/B test messaging campaigns aimed at delivering more personalized in-game experiences using R, SQL, and HiveQL.


    \item Identified player segments most likely to respond positively to upsell messaging, contributing to a 3\% cohort spend increase.


    \item Analysis and recommendations from in-game messaging experiments generated an additional \$2M in revenue for FIFA 19.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

{\vspace{-0.5cm}}

\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2016-06 -- \\ 2018-11}
  {Business Analyst, FIFA Player Analytics Team}
  {Burnaby, BC Canada}
  {Owned executive reporting, player engagement and monetization analysis, and licensing analytics for FIFA — one of EA's highest-revenue franchises — while extending analytical coverage to Motive, BioWare, and DICE studios. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Managed game performance and insight delivery for monthly executive board meetings for EA Sports titles, and became trusted with reporting support for Motive, BioWare, and DICE studios.


    \item Owned analytics reporting for FIFA's strategic licensing partners and automated large portions of the process using R and Talend, reducing weeks of manual work assembling 550 slides to seconds.


    \item Applied propensity score matching in R to measure the true engagement and spend lift from FIFA's Companion and Web Apps, providing the evidence base that saved the Web App from discontinuation and secured ongoing investment in both platforms.

    \begin{itemize}

      \item Incremental spending gains among spenders of 4.2\% for Web App users and 7.0\% for Mobile App users, leading to millions in incremental revenue.

      \item Presented findings at EA's 2018 Analyticon conference.

      \item Findings and follow-on performance helped influence the Madden team to develop a companion app for Madden 19.

    \end{itemize}


    \item Built statistical models measuring the revenue impact each sports license generated for EA and the brand exposure value returned to partners, informing smarter procurement and renewal decisions.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

\section{TinyMob Games - Indie Game Startup \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/tinymob_games_logo.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2014-11 -- \\ 2015-07}
  {Data Analyst, TinyRealms}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Early-career analyst role focused on KPI reporting, player retention analysis, and monetization diagnosis for TinyMob's first mobile game, Tiny Realms. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Discovered a segment of players with elevated churn likelihood, attributed it to unbalanced gameplay, and worked with game design on solutions that improved early game (D7) retention from 11.3\% to 14.2\% for the affected cohort.


    \item Discovered that one game-mode cannibalized revenue by reducing the impact of the game's soft paywall, leading to design changes that remedied it.


    \item Uncovered a free-rider problem and identified it as the primary driver of the studio's monetization challenges.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}
